stance: Risk-balanced value-with-rising-revisions; overweight cheap dividend cash-cows (energy/PSU/financials) where analysts are upgrading, exit the names where revisions have collapsed.
experience_note: This is an early round with no graded track record yet, so these theses are pre-registered for honest scoring next round. I am watching three things: (1) whether the cheap-and-upgrading cohort (ONGC, NMDC, LICI, Gujarat Gas, Chennai Petro) actually outperforms the cheap-and-downgrading cohort I exited (HINDPETRO, BPCL, MGL) — that is the direct test of the value_revision thesis; (2) whether refusing STLNETWORK and the broken-z_val microcaps costs me or saves me versus the quant baseline that bought them; (3) whether my decision to size winners bigger shows positive conviction-IC. What would change my mind: if the high-revision energy/PSU names underperform the low-revision ones over the next 1-3 months, that refutes treating analyst revisions as a useful tilt at all and I would revert closer to a pure-cheapness book.
